lede: A look at August 19 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Bill Clinton and Manuel L. Quezon, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Google
events.desc: Google Inc. has its initial public offering on Nasdaq.

events.title: Canal Hotel bombing
events.desc: A truck-bomb attack on United Nations headquarters in Iraq kills the agency's top envoy Sérgio Vieira de Mello and 21 other employees.

events.title: Belgrade
events.desc: In Belgrade, Yugoslavia, tens of thousands of Serbians rally to demand the resignation of Federal Republic of Yugoslavia President Slobodan Milošević.

events.title: Cold War
events.desc: Cold War: The CIA and MI6 help to overthrow the government of Mohammad Mosaddegh in Iran and reinstate the Shah Mohammad Reza Pahlavi.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Operation Jubilee (The Dieppe Raid): The 2nd Canadian Infantry Division leads an amphibious assault by allied forces on Dieppe, France and fails.

events.title: Nazi Germany
events.desc: Germany and Romania sign the Tiraspol Agreement, rendering the region of Transnistria under control of the latter.

events.title: 1934 German head of state referendum
events.desc: The German referendum of 1934 approves Adolf Hitler's appointment as head of state with the title of Führer.

events.title: Charles Edward Stuart
events.desc: Prince Charles Edward Stuart raises his standard in Glenfinnan: The start of the Second Jacobite Rebellion, known as "the 45".

events.title: Johann Sebastian Bach
events.desc: J. S. Bach leads the first performance of Lobe den Herren, den mächtigen König der Ehren, BWV 137, a cantata setting the unchanged text of Neander's hymn.

events.title: Julius Caesar
events.desc: In the wake of the murder of Julius Caesar, his nephew and heir, Octavian, uses the threat of military force to compel the Roman Senate to elect him Consul.

births.name: Bill Clinton
births.line: American lawyer and politician, 40th and 42nd Governor of Arkansas, 42nd President of the United States

births.name: Manuel L. Quezon
births.line: Filipino soldier, lawyer, and politician, 2nd President of the Philippines

births.name: Íñigo López de Mendoza, 1st Marquis of Santillana
Wright brothers
births.line: American engineer and pilot, co-founded the Wright Company

deaths.text: Linus Pauling, American chemist and biologist, Nobel Prize laureate
Otto Frank, German-Swiss businessman, father of Anne Frank
Federico García Lorca, Spanish poet, playwright, and director
Blaise Pascal, French mathematician, physicist, and philosopher

observances.text: Afghan Independence Day, commemorates the Treaty of Rawalpindi in 1919, granting independence from Britain (Afghanistan)
Christian Feast Day: Ezequiél Moreno y Díaz
Christian Feast Day: Feast of the Transfiguration (Julian calendar), and its related observances: Buhe (Ethiopian Orthodox Tewahedo Church and Eritrean Orthodox Tewahedo Church)
